summary: Adjusted JObj inline definitions and matrix-access naming after discovering that HSD_JObjSetupMatrix failed to inline into lbcoll and that the old HSD_JObjGetMtx helper was really a matrix-pointer getter. The PR replaced an asm MUST_MATCH copy in lbcollision with a C wrapper matching the inlined setup-matrix behavior, renamed HSD_JObjGetMtx to HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr with a MtxPtr return, and used that helper to simplify/improve matches in fighter and Purin code.
what_changed: Changed HSD_JObjSetupMatrix in jobj.h from a static inline with a positive conditional to an inline function with early return: return when jobj is NULL or HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty is false, otherwise call HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub.
Renamed/redefined HSD_JObjGetMtx as HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, returning MtxPtr instead of void. The helper still asserts the JObj, calls lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix, then returns jobj->mtx.
Updated ftPr_Init_UnkMtxFunc0 to use MtxPtr and HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr directly, removing a MUST_MATCH unused stack workaround and the manual `(0, &bone_jobj->mtx)` pattern.
Updated Fighter_UnkApplyTransformation_8006C0F0 to pass HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr(jobj) into HSD_MtxInverse, removing comments and temporary code that existed to work around inline/stack matching behavior.

In lbcollision.c, replaced an inline PowerPC asm MUST_MATCH implementation of lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix with C code using a new static inline lbColl_JObjMtxIsDirty helper and a C lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix wrapper.
Changed lbColl_804D3700 and lbColl_804D3708 extern declarations from `char*` to fixed char arrays of size 8, matching their use as assertion strings/data.
Updated local matrix helpers in lbcollision, including mtxConcat and pickMtx, to use HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr and MtxPtr instead of taking addresses of jobj->mtx through temporary HSD_JObj variables.
Adjusted lbColl_80008248 call site so pickMtx returns the matrix pointer directly instead of requiring dereference of an Mtx pointer.
smart_moves: Used the failed inline of HSD_JObjSetupMatrix into lbcoll as evidence that the header definition shape needed to change rather than forcing more local matching hacks.
Recognized that HSD_JObjGetMtx was misnamed: its observable purpose was to prepare and return access to the JObj matrix pointer, not merely perform a void getter side effect.
Converted awkward decomp patterns like `HSD_JObjGetMtx(jobj); jobj->mtx` and `(0, &bone_jobj->mtx)` into a typed helper, reducing fake temporaries and unused stack padding.

Kept the lbcoll-specific lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix symbol as a temporary stand-in because linker deduplication apparently kept the lbcoll definition, while documenting that it should be renamed back once lbcoll is matched.
Removed a large MUST_MATCH asm block by expressing the same dirty-matrix predicate and setup call in C, improving maintainability while preserving the matching intent.
decomp_lessons: If an inline fails to appear in a deeply nested function, compare the local emitted code against the header inline shape; small control-flow differences such as early return versus nested conditionals can decide whether matches work.
A helper that exists mainly for its side effect may still need to return a value if original code used the prepared field immediately afterward; naming should reflect the actual value flow.
Prefer using project typedefs such as MtxPtr when the code is passing matrix pointers around. This avoids misleading `Mtx*` usage and can eliminate artificial dereferences.
Manual comma-expression/address patterns and unused stack MUST_MATCH pads are signs that a missing inline or wrong helper signature may be the real problem.
When a decompiled inline depends on an lbcoll-local copy because of linker deduplication, keep the temporary symbol explicit and document the eventual rename path rather than hiding the dependency.
Reconstructing small inline predicates, such as JObj matrix dirty checks, can remove asm while preserving the exact call gate into lower-level routines like HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub.

naming_conventions.reason: The helper returns `jobj->mtx` as an MtxPtr after asserting/setup, so `GetMtxPtr` better describes the value returned.

naming_conventions.name: lbColl_JObjMtxIsDirty
naming_conventions.reason: Local lbcoll copy of the JObj dirty-matrix predicate used to mirror the inlined setup-matrix behavior.

naming_conventions.name: lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix
naming_conventions.reason: Kept as a temporary lbcoll-specific symbol because the PR notes the inline was kept there by linker deduplication; comments say to rename it back to HSD_JObjSetupMatrix once lbcoll is matched.

naming_conventions.name: MtxPtr
naming_conventions.reason: Preferred type for matrix pointer returns and parameters where code passes the matrix buffer directly.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced a full MUST_MATCH asm version of lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix with C by matching the assert, dirty-flag predicate, early return, and HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub call.
Changed HSD_JObjSetupMatrix control flow to early-return form, which better matched the lbcoll inlined copy.
Used HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr to combine matrix setup and pointer retrieval, improving matches in melee/ft according to the PR body.
Removed unused stack padding in ftPr_Init_UnkMtxFunc0 after the correct matrix pointer helper made the match cleaner.
Removed temporary-variable/address-taking patterns around jobj->mtx in fighter and lbcollision to better express original pointer flow.

The evidence mentions linker deduplication keeping the lbcoll definition, so the PR temporarily exposes lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix from jobj.h despite noting that jobj.h should not permanently depend on lbcollision.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbColl_JObjSetupMatrix back to HSD_JObjSetupMatrix after lbcoll matching.
Search for remaining uses of HSD_JObjGetMtx or manual `jobj->mtx` access that should become HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr.
Check whether other JObj inline helpers have control-flow shapes that prevent inlining into deeply nested functions.
Look for remaining MUST_MATCH unused stack pads near matrix helpers; they may indicate missing inline return values or wrong typedefs.
